questions.q: What is the primary role of an Event Manager in the management of sporting events?
questions.answer: An Event Manager is responsible for planning, organizing, and overseeing the execution of a sporting event.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The ______________ is the most critical phase of event management, where all the preparations are made.
Which of the following is NOT a function of an Event Manager? (a) Budgeting (b) Marketing (c) Accounting (d) Coaching
Explain any TWO functions of an Event Manager during the pre-event phase.
questions.answer: Functions of an Event Manager during the pre-event phase include (i) Planning and Budgeting, and (ii) Marketing and Promotion.

questions.q: True or False: An Event Manager is responsible for the execution of the event only.
What is meant by 'Risk Management' in the context of event management? State its importance.
questions.answer: Risk Management refers to identifying and mitigating potential risks that may affect the smooth conduct of an event. Its importance lies in minimizing losses and ensuring the success of the event.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The ______________ Phase is the most critical phase of event management, where all the preparations are made.

Which of the following is a characteristic of a successful Event Manager? (a) Ability to make decisions quickly (b) Willingness to take risks (c) Ability to communicate effectively (d) All of the above
Explain the importance of 'Communication' in the management of sporting events.
questions.answer: Communication is crucial in the management of sporting events as it helps in conveying information to stakeholders, including participants, officials, and spectators. Effective communication ensures that all parties are aware of their roles and responsibilities, leading to a smooth and successful event.

questions.q: True or False: An Event Manager is responsible for the marketing and promotion of the event only.
